annotations.label: Automorphic form (factor of automorphy)

anchor.snippet: is termed an automorphic form if the following holds

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes only the modular-forms special case (SL(2,ℝ) acting on ℍ with the weight-k slash action `(cz+d)^k`) via `SlashInvariantForm`, not the general factor-of-automorphy definition on an arbitrary group/space.

annotations.label: Factor of automorphy

anchor.snippet: The factor of automorphy for the automorphic form

annotations.note: Only the specific weight-k cocycle `(cz+d)^k` for GL(2,ℝ) on ℍ is formalized, as the `SlashAction` typeclass; there is no general factor-of-automorphy / 1-cocycle abstraction.

annotations.label: Automorphic function

anchor.snippet: An automorphic function is an automorphic form for which

annotations.note: Closest match is a weight-0 `SlashInvariantForm` (a function on ℍ invariant under a subgroup of GL(2,ℝ)); the general notion of an automorphic function with trivial factor of automorphy on arbitrary groups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Automorphic form (three conditions)

anchor.snippet: subject to three kinds of conditions

annotations.note: The general Lie-group definition imposing discrete-subgroup invariance, moderate growth, and K-finiteness/Z(g)-finiteness is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Eisenstein series as automorphic form

anchor.snippet: which is a prototypical modular form

annotations.note: `eisensteinSeriesMF` packages the weight-k Eisenstein series as a `ModularForm` for congruence subgroups.

annotations.label: Generalized Dirichlet L-functions

anchor.snippet: Specific generalizations of Dirichlet L-functions as class field-theoretic objects

annotations.note: Mathlib has ordinary Dirichlet `LFunction` (Mathlib.NumberTheory.LSeries.DirichletContinuation) but not the class-field-theoretic generalizations (Hecke/Größencharacter L-functions) referenced here.

annotations.label: Harmonic analytic functor on Galois groups

anchor.snippet: Generally any harmonic analytic object as a functor over Galois groups

annotations.note: No Mathlib formalization of harmonic-analytic functors on Galois groups in the Langlands sense.

annotations.label: Automorphic representation

anchor.snippet: an automorphic representation is a representation that is an infinite tensor product of representations of p-adic groups

annotations.note: Automorphic representations of adelic groups (restricted tensor products of local representations) are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Poincaré's automorphic function

anchor.section: Poincaré on discovery and his work on automorphic functions
anchor.snippet: an automorphic function is one which is analytic in its domain and is invariant under a discrete infinite group of linear fractional transformations

annotations.note: A weight-0 holomorphic `SlashInvariantForm` for a discrete subgroup of SL(2,ℝ) realises Poincaré's notion, but Mathlib does not define automorphic functions for arbitrary discrete Möbius/Fuchsian groups in general.
